description: Add E2E tests for the two confirmed coverage gaps: browser_export_har tool (never functionally tested) and the permission/confirmation system (fully implemented with zero E2E coverage).

userStories.title: E2E test for browser_export_har tool
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests for browser_export_har so that HAR export functionality is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: A test enables network capture, navigates to generate traffic, calls browser_export_har, and verifies the response contains valid HAR 1.2 JSON with entries
A test verifies browser_export_har with includeWebSocketFrames: true includes WebSocket frame entries
A test verifies browser_export_har with clear: true empties the capture buffer (subsequent get_network_requests returns empty)
A test verifies browser_export_har returns an error when called on a tab without active network capture
Tests are added to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts following the existing network capture test patterns
userStories.notes:
Add tests to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts in the existing 'Browser tools — network capture lifecycle' describe block (around line 1641). Follow the exact pattern of existing tests:

1. `initAndListTools()` to set up
2. `openTestServerTab()` to get a tabId
3. `browser_enable_network_capture` with `{ tabId }`
4. `browser_navigate_tab` to `testServer.url + '/interactive'` to generate HTTP traffic
5. `waitForNetworkRequests()` to ensure data is captured before exporting
6. Call `browser_export_har` with `{ tabId }`
7. Parse result: `const harJson = JSON.parse(parseToolResult(result.content).har)`
8. Verify HAR 1.2 structure: `harJson.log.version === '1.2'`, `harJson.log.entries.length > 0`, entries have `request.url`, `request.method`, `response.status`

For WebSocket frames test: the test server has a WebSocket endpoint at `testServer.url` (see e2e/test-server.ts). Navigate to a page, open a WS connection via browser_execute_script, send a message, then export HAR with `includeWebSocketFrames: true`. Verify entries with synthetic WS markers exist.

For the clear test: export with `{ tabId, clear: true }`, then call `browser_get_network_requests` and verify empty array.

For the error test: call export_har on a tab without enable_network_capture — verify isError response.

The tool implementation is at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts. The helper `waitForNetworkRequests()` is at e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts:141. The helper `waitForWebSocketFrames()` is at e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts:170.

userStories.title: E2E test for permission evaluation — observe tier auto-allows, interact/sensitive tier triggers confirmation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests that verify the permission tier system evaluates correctly — observe tools auto-allow, interact/sensitive tools require confirmation on non-trusted domains.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test file e2e/permissions.e2e.ts exists with tests for the permission system
A fixture or helper starts the MCP server WITHOUT skipConfirmation (env var OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION must NOT be set)
A test calls an observe-tier tool (e.g. browser_list_tabs) and verifies it succeeds without any confirmation request

A test calls an interact-tier tool (e.g. browser_click_element) on a non-trusted domain and verifies the MCP client receives a progress notification containing 'approval' while the tool is pending confirmation
The test server serves pages on a non-localhost domain (e.g. 127.0.0.2 or a custom hostname) so it is NOT in the default trustedDomains list
userStories.notes:
Create e2e/permissions.e2e.ts. The key challenge is running the server without skipConfirmation.

The fixture at e2e/fixtures.ts line 503 sets `OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION: '1'`. To override, use the `envOverrides` parameter (4th arg to `startMcpServer`, line 462) — either extend the existing `mcpServer` fixture or create a local helper that starts a server with `{ OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION: '' }` (empty string to unset). Setting to empty string or `'0'` should work since the check is `process.env.OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION === '1'` (see platform/mcp-server/src/skip-confirmation.ts).

The default trusted domains are `['localhost', '127.0.0.1']` (platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts line 124-128). So the test server at `localhost:PORT` will auto-allow everything. To test confirmation flow, the test needs a tab open on a NON-trusted domain. Options:
- Bind the test server to `127.0.0.2` (loopback alias, works on macOS/Linux without config)
- Or use `0.0.0.0` and navigate the browser to `http://127.0.0.2:PORT/`
- Or configure custom trustedDomains in config.json to NOT include localhost

Best approach: start the test server bound to `0.0.0.0`, then navigate to `http://127.0.0.2:PORT/app`. Since `127.0.0.2` is not in the default trustedDomains, interact/sensitive tools will require confirmation.

For the observe-tier test: call `browser_list_tabs` (observe tier, auto-allow) — it should succeed immediately without any confirmation.

For the confirmation-pending test: call `browser_click_element` on a tab at 127.0.0.2 — the tool will block waiting for confirmation. The MCP client should receive a progress notification. Use the MCP client's progress callback (see e2e/fixtures.ts `createMcpClient` — it stores progress events). Verify a notification arrives with text mentioning 'approval'. Then the tool will timeout after 30s if not responded to — the test should verify the pending state, then cancel/timeout.

Permission evaluation: platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts:121 (`evaluatePermission()`). Tool tiers: lines 29-74 (TOOL_TIERS map). Confirmation request: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts:217 (`sendConfirmationRequest()`). Progress notification 'Waiting for human approval': platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts (search for 'approval').

IMPORTANT: Do not add too many tests. Focus on the core tier evaluation — observe auto-allows, interact/sensitive triggers confirmation pending state. The next stories handle the confirmation dialog response flow.

userStories.title: E2E test for confirmation dialog — Allow Once flow
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want an E2E test that exercises the full Allow Once confirmation flow — from MCP tool call through side panel dialog to tool completion.
acceptanceCriteria: A test calls a sensitive-tier browser tool on a non-trusted domain without skipConfirmation
The test opens the side panel, detects the confirmation dialog appearing with the correct tool name and domain
The test clicks 'Allow Once' in the confirmation dialog
The tool call completes successfully after the confirmation is granted
A subsequent call to the same tool triggers another confirmation dialog (Allow Once does NOT persist)
userStories.notes:
Add to e2e/permissions.e2e.ts created in US-002.

The flow:
1. Start MCP server without skipConfirmation (use the helper from US-002)
2. Launch extension, open test app on 127.0.0.2 (non-trusted)
3. Open side panel via `openSidePanel()` helper (e2e/helpers.ts)
4. Call a sensitive tool (e.g. `browser_get_cookies`) via mcpClient — this will BLOCK waiting for confirmation
5. In parallel (Promise.all or race), poll the side panel page for the ConfirmationDialog:
   - Look for text 'Approval Required' or the tool name in the side panel DOM
   - The dialog component is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx
   - 'Allow Once' button has text 'Allow Once' (line 138)
6. Click 'Allow Once' button in the side panel
7. The tool call resolves successfully

The tricky part is timing: the tool call blocks, so the test must interact with the side panel concurrently. Use `Promise.all([toolCallPromise, interactWithDialogPromise])` pattern.

For the non-persistence test: after the first call completes, call the same tool again. Verify a new confirmation dialog appears (Allow Once only permits one call).

The side panel URL pattern is `chrome-extension://<id>/side-panel/side-panel.html` — see `openSidePanel()` in e2e/helpers.ts. The confirmation dialog sends `confirmation.response` via chrome.runtime.sendMessage (platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts), which the background relays to the server via WebSocket (platform/browser-extension/src/background/background-message-handlers.ts).

The MCP client's `callTool()` returns a Promise that resolves when the tool completes. The confirmation dialog has a 30s timeout (CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT_MS in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts:189). The test should complete well within that window.

userStories.title: E2E test for confirmation dialog — Deny flow and Allow Always session persistence
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests for the Deny and Allow Always confirmation flows to verify that denied tools return permission errors and Allow Always persists for the session.
acceptanceCriteria: A test calls a browser tool requiring confirmation, clicks 'Deny' in the side panel dialog, and verifies the tool call returns an error with permission denied indication
A test calls a browser tool requiring confirmation, clicks 'Allow Always' (tool_domain scope), and verifies the tool completes successfully
After Allow Always, a subsequent call to the same tool on the same domain succeeds immediately without triggering another confirmation dialog
userStories.notes:
Add to e2e/permissions.e2e.ts.

Deny flow:
1. Same setup as US-003 — server without skipConfirmation, tab on 127.0.0.2
2. Call a sensitive tool (e.g. `browser_execute_script`)
3. In parallel, find the confirmation dialog in the side panel, click 'Deny'
4. The tool call should resolve with isError=true. Check for 'PERMISSION_DENIED' or similar in the error content. The error is generated in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts — search for 'PERMISSION_DENIED'.

Allow Always flow:
1. Same setup
2. Call a tool requiring confirmation
3. In the side panel, click the 'Allow Always' dropdown, select 'For this tool on this domain' (scope: tool_domain)
4. Tool completes successfully
5. Call the SAME tool on the SAME domain again
6. This time it should complete immediately WITHOUT a confirmation dialog appearing
7. Verify by checking that no new ConfirmationDialog appears within ~2 seconds (the tool should resolve quickly)

The 'Allow Always' dropdown in the side panel: the button has class or text 'Allow Always', and clicking it reveals scope options. The 'For this tool on this domain' option triggers `onRespond(id, 'allow_always', 'tool_domain')`. See ConfirmationDialog.tsx lines 56-70.

Session persistence is managed by `state.sessionPermissions` array in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts and checked by `isSessionAllowed()` in platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts before evaluatePermission() is called.

userStories.title: E2E test for trusted domains auto-allow and browserToolPolicy disable
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests verifying that trusted domains bypass confirmation and that browserToolPolicy can disable tools entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: A test calls an interact-tier tool on a trusted domain (localhost) without skipConfirmation and verifies it succeeds immediately without confirmation
A test configures browserToolPolicy to disable a specific browser tool, verifies it disappears from tools/list, and verifies calling it returns an error
userStories.notes:
Add to e2e/permissions.e2e.ts.

Trusted domain test:
1. Start server without skipConfirmation, use the same fixture from US-002
2. Open a tab on localhost (the test server's default binding) — localhost IS in the default trustedDomains
3. Call an interact-tier tool (e.g. `browser_click_element`, `browser_navigate_tab`) on that tab
4. The tool should succeed immediately without triggering any confirmation dialog
5. To verify no dialog appeared: check that the tool resolved within a short time (< 2s), and/or check the side panel has no ConfirmationDialog

This tests the trustedDomains upgrade from 'ask' to 'allow' (platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts line ~155).

browserToolPolicy test:
1. Create a config with `browserToolPolicy: { 'browser_screenshot_tab': false }` in the config.json
2. Start the server, create MCP client
3. Call tools/list — verify `browser_screenshot_tab` is NOT in the list
4. Call `browser_screenshot_tab` directly — verify it returns an error
5. The policy is checked in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts:345 (excluded from tools/list) and platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts:174 (rejected on call)

This test does NOT need extension or side panel — it's purely server-side. Can use a simpler setup (just mcpServer + mcpClient).

IMPORTANT: This is the final story and e2eCheckpoint. The full E2E suite must pass.
